entries.speaker: Jo
entries.text: Welcome back. Today let's talk about the mobile app rewrite.

entries.speaker: Tariq
entries.text: The React Native prototype hits 60fps on mid-range devices now. The bridge overhead concerns turned out to be manageable.

entries.speaker: Lena
entries.text: I'm not convinced a full rewrite is the right call. We could incrementally migrate screen by screen inside the existing native shell.

entries.speaker: Tariq
entries.text: Incremental migration means maintaining two build systems for a year. The team is small.

entries.speaker: Lena
entries.text: A big-bang rewrite has killed more apps than slow builds have. But if we timebox it hard, fair enough.

entries.speaker: Jo
entries.text: We agreed to a timeboxed six-month rewrite with a go or no-go review at month three.

entries.speaker: Jo
entries.text: Okay, moving on to the offline mode.

entries.speaker: Lena
entries.text: Offline is the top request from field teams. Sync conflicts are the hard part, not storage.

entries.speaker: Tariq
entries.text: Last-write-wins is simple and covers ninety percent of cases. CRDTs are overkill for form data.

entries.speaker: Lena
entries.text: Last-write-wins with a conflict log the user can review. Works for me.

entries.speaker: Jo
entries.text: So we decided to build offline mode with last-write-wins sync and a visible conflict log.

entries.speaker: Tariq
entries.text: Three enterprise customers are blocked on it. They want webhooks and a read API at minimum.

entries.speaker: Lena
entries.text: I don't think we should promise a full plugin runtime this year. Webhooks and a read API are a quarter of the work and most of the value.

entries.speaker: Tariq
entries.text: Fair enough, runtime can wait. Webhooks first.

entries.speaker: Jo
entries.text: Let's go with webhooks plus the read API for Q4, full plugin runtime revisited next year.

entries.speaker: Jo
entries.text: Good session. I'll write up the roadmap doc.
